name: Global Compliance Packs Store
description: Public storefront selling framework-specific compliance document kits (ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR, DPDP, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, NIST CSF) via Razorpay, delivered by a human within 24 hours.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-11. frontend/index.html:6534 renders <section id="compliance-global"> ("Global Compliance" grid, #compliance-grid at line 6556) — the data-auth-gate="true" attribute and its display:none default have been removed, so the section now renders for every visitor. window.purchaseCompliancePack() (~line 6787) calls POST /api/global/compliance-packs/purchase (line 6811) then /verify (line 6839) on Razorpay success, unchanged.

navigation.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-11. Primary nav link (frontend/index.html:1232, '🌐 Global Compliance') is visible to every visitor and now reaches a genuinely visible section — confirmed via cdbNavigate()'s own auth-gate check (frontend/index.html:14714-14722): with no data-auth-gate attribute present, that function no longer redirects the visitor back to hero/home, matching the backend's own no-auth-required purchase flow (handlePurchaseCompliancePack never reads its authCtx parameter; handleVerifyCompliancePack isn't even passed one at its call site, workers/src/index.js:6563).

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/complianceGlobalPublicAccess.test.mjs (new, 2026-07-11, 5 tests): the section carries no data-auth-gate attribute, carries no display:none default, the nav link and purchase flow are unchanged, and sibling gated sections (executive-hub, growth-analytics) are untouched by this fix. Full backend suite green: 207 files / 2149 tests. scripts/seo-structure-lock.mjs: all 22 pages green (unaffected, change is inside <body>). CI's Accessibility (axe) check (a required job) then caught a real, pre-existing WCAG 'color-contrast' violation on 3 of the 7 compliance-pack badge spans (soc2 #4f46e5, gdpr #2563eb, nist_csf #ef4444) that had never been auditable before, because the section was display:none and axe-core does not evaluate hidden content. Verified the exact ratios by reproducing axe-core's own WCAG relative-luminance/contrast-ratio algorithm in a small Node script against the composited badge background (section #080818 + card rgba(255,255,255,.03) + badge rgba(255,255,255,.06) ≈ rgb(29,29,44)): soc2 2.642:1, gdpr 3.214:1, nist_csf 4.414:1 — all below the 4.5:1 AA minimum for the badge's 10px text, matching axe's report exactly. Fixed by adding a badgeColor field (lighter same-hue tints: soc2→#818cf8, gdpr→#60a5fa, nist_csf→#f87171) used only for the badge span's text color (color:${p.badgeColor || p.color}); price text, badge border, and hover-border color all keep the original brand color unchanged, since those were not flagged and the price text's larger bold font already clears the more lenient 3:1 large-text threshold against the darker card background. Recomputed ratios for the 3 replacements: 5.568:1, 6.533:1, 6.004:1 — all comfortably above 4.5:1; the 4 untouched packs (iso27001, dpdp, hipaa, pci_dss) were already 6.1:1+ and remain unchanged.

verification.evidence: First audit of this domain (previously an unpopulated [] stub, flagged in docs/PRODUCTION_GAP_CLOSURE_MASTER_PROMPT.md as never-audited). Discovered via background Explore agent, cross-checked directly: workers/src/services/globalScale.js (COMPLIANCE_PACKS catalog, 3 handlers) and workers/src/index.js:6549-6567 read directly; frontend/index.html read directly for both the nav entry (line 1232) and the gated section (line 6534) and the auth-gate comment (lines 406-407); workers/test/*.mjs grepped with 0 relevant hits. FIXED 2026-07-11 (same-day fix, PR #166): removed the data-auth-gate="true" attribute and display:none default from the section; also fixed a real WCAG color-contrast violation CI's axe check caught on 3 of 7 badges once the section became visible (see test_coverage.evidence). DEPLOYED + RE-VERIFIED ON LIVE PRODUCTION 2026-07-11: PR #166 merged (squash 6ca6fb7), all 32 checks green including Accessibility (axe). Polled https://cyberdudebivash.in until the deployed markup showed the gate removed, then drove a real headless-Chromium session against live production with no login of any kind: confirmed #compliance-global renders with computed display:block and real height, all 7 pack badges are present, and the 3 fixed badges' computed colors (rgb(129,140,248)/rgb(96,165,250)/rgb(248,113,113)) match the shipped fix exactly. Zero JS errors. customer_journey_complete stays false — this verifies discoverability/visibility, not a completed purchase; that would require a real Razorpay transaction, which was not attempted.

notes: Real, live, revenue-integrated Razorpay flow — not a stub. The same conceptual product (an ISO 27001 pack) is sold at three different prices through three separate, non-cross-referencing backend code paths: ₹4,999 here, ₹3,499 via workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js's Sentinel APEX 'cp-iso27001-2022' line item (see CAP-COMP-002's notes), and ₹999 via toolsMarketplace.js's 'compliance_starter_pack' (CAP-COMP-003). No deliverable is ever generated automatically — fulfillment is a manual, human, within-24-hours process (workers/src/services/globalScale.js:408,462,475). Priority kept at P1 despite being fixed in this same pass — the registry records what was found and corrected, not just the end state, matching this session's established convention (e.g. CAP-MSSP-001, CAP-IDN-001/002/003).

name: Compliance Assessment / Readiness Engine (CDB-COMP-001)
description: A paid, detailed compliance gap-assessment engine (93 ISO 27001:2022 Annex A controls across 4 themes, NIST CSF 2.0 6-function scoring, GDPR article-level assessment, phased certification roadmap) — materially deeper than the free scanner catalogued as CAP-SCAN-005, reachable via a direct tier-gated scan route and via the generic service-order/marketplace fulfillment engine.

frontend.evidence: grep -rn "scan/compliance\b" frontend/*.html returns 0 matches; no frontend file calls POST /api/services/orders with a compliance-engine reference. (A structurally distinct product-catalog entry point into the same runComplianceAssessment() engine does have a frontend caller — workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js's Sentinel APEX 'compliance_pack' category, cp-nist-csf-2/cp-iso27001-2022 — but that belongs to the already-populated sentinel-apex-marketplace domain, not here; see notes.)

navigation.evidence: No nav entry or page links to /api/scan/compliance or a compliance-flavored /api/services/orders flow anywhere in frontend/.

test_coverage.evidence: grep -rn "handleComplianceScan" workers/test/*.mjs returns 0 matches. workers/test/serviceOrderPaymentGate.test.mjs imports createServiceOrder for real and tests the payment-gate logic generically, but its fixture services map (lines 24-33) only defines CDB-SSL-001 and CDB-CONSULT-001 — the compliance engine dispatch path itself is untested.

verification.evidence: workers/src/services/complianceEngine.js:238 (runComplianceAssessment, header comment line 3 labels it 'CDB-COMP-001 (₹24,999)'), workers/src/handlers/serviceHandlers.js:218-227 (handleComplianceScan, hard 403 unless authCtx.isAdmin or tier in ['PRO','ENTERPRISE']), workers/src/services/serviceOrderEngine.js:31,68,103-105,155,324 (ENGINE_DISPATCH.compliance, auto-dispatch tier gate) all read directly. frontend/*.html and workers/test/*.mjs grepped with the results in the fields above.

notes: Real naming collision worth tracking: this file's runComplianceAssessment() is a completely different, much deeper engine than the one CAP-SCAN-005 uses (workers/src/engine.js), despite both domains using the word 'compliance' throughout. A shipped, tier-gated, ₹24,999 paid capability with zero frontend caller and zero test coverage is invisible to every customer today regardless of what they pay for. Cross-domain note (not claimed here): workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js's MARKETPLACE_CATALOG has a 'compliance_pack' category (cp-nist-csf-2 ₹2,499, cp-iso27001-2022 ₹3,499) that also calls runComplianceAssessment() on delivery (line ~637) and DOES have a real frontend caller (frontend/index.html, frontend/marketplace-checkout.html) and real test coverage (workers/test/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.test.mjs:306). docs/capability-registry/domains/sentinel-apex-marketplace.json was checked directly for 'compliance_pack'/'NIST CSF'/'cp-nist' — zero matches, meaning that specific line item isn't itemized in that domain's registry entry either. Recommend a follow-up pass on the sentinel-apex-marketplace domain to add that line item's evidence, rather than duplicating it here.

name: Tools Marketplace Compliance Toolkits
description: A third, separate low-price compliance product catalog (compliance_starter_pack ₹999, dpdp_compliance_kit ₹699, plus a compliance pack bundled into enterprise_security_bundle ₹4,499) sold from the homepage 'tools' section.

frontend.evidence: window.cdbHomeBuy(productId, priceInr, productName) (~line 6966) invoked via onclick="cdbHomeBuy('compliance_starter_pack',999,...)" (line 7027) and onclick="cdbHomeBuy('dpdp_compliance_kit',699,...)" (line 7031), in a homepage tools section separate from CAP-COMP-001's 'Global Compliance' section.

navigation.evidence: Homepage section, no auth-gate wrapper found around it (unlike CAP-COMP-001) — reachable by any visitor who scrolls the homepage.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/toolsMarketplace.js:33,37,40,48,53,95 read directly (catalog entries + 3 handlers); workers/src/index.js:6586,6592 read directly, both routes explicitly commented 'public'; frontend/index.html cdbHomeBuy() call sites at lines 6966,7027,7031 read directly; workers/test/*.mjs grepped with 0 hits.

notes: Backend, frontend, and navigation all genuinely exist and are wired — the sole real gap is zero test coverage of an unauthenticated public payment-adjacent endpoint. See CAP-COMP-001's notes for the 3-way ISO-27001-pack price fragmentation this catalog contributes a third price point to.

name: DPDP Act 2023 Compliance Management Engine
description: A dedicated engine for India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 — section-by-section gap analysis, readiness scoring, and Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) generation — distinct from CAP-SCAN-005's generic multi-framework scanner and from CAP-COMP-002's ISO/NIST/GDPR engine.

frontend.evidence: frontend/compliance-management.html — the page whose marketing copy most resembles this feature ('Multi-framework compliance management', 'automated evidence collection', lines 7-8) — was checked explicitly for 'dpdp' (case-insensitive) and for every fetch/axios/XHR call: its only live API call (line 146) hits /api/generate/compliance, the already-catalogued CAP-SCAN-005 route. No frontend file anywhere calls any /api/compliance/dpdp/* route.

navigation.evidence: Not applicable — no frontend page reaches this backend at all, so there is nothing to navigate to.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/dpdpCompliance.js read directly in full: tier gate confirmed real (not just a doc-comment claim) at lines 156-157 (handleDPDPOverview), 198-199 (handleDPDPAssess), 331-332 (handleDPDPSections), 354-355 (handleDPDPRoPA) — all hard-403 unless tier is PRO/ENTERPRISE/MSSP; handleDPDPReports/handleDPDPReport (lines 426,446) require only authentication (any tier), scoped by user_id, which is correctly safe since a sub-PRO user can never have created a report to list. workers/src/index.js:3776-3798 read directly for all 6 routes. frontend/compliance-management.html read directly and grepped for 'dpdp' and for fetch/axios calls (0 matches for either). workers/test/*.mjs grepped with 0 hits.

notes: Fully backend-only today: a real, tier-gated, non-trivial engine (RoPA generation, section-level gap analysis) that no customer can reach through any UI. frontend/compliance-management.html's marketing copy ('automated evidence collection', 'continuous compliance monitoring', 'compliance as code') overstates what any backend capability in this domain actually does — every engine found in this audit (this one, CAP-COMP-002, and CAP-SCAN-005) is a one-off, on-demand gap assessment, not continuous monitoring or automated evidence collection. That page is linked from frontend/sitemap.html (lines 122, 208) and cross-linked from several other marketing pages, so the overclaim is genuinely customer-facing, not dead content. Precedent: workers/test/proposalGeneratorHonestClaims.test.mjs documents a prior, since-fixed incident of the proposal generator falsely claiming in-progress SOC 2/ISO 27001 certification — the same category of risk.

name: Trust Center Compliance Framework Alignment
description: A public, honestly-scoped list of which compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, DPDP, OWASP LLM, MITRE, NIST AI) the platform aligns with vs. holds formal certification for.

routes_sampled.evidence: workers/src/index.js:5304-5306 (handleEnterpriseTrustCenter, an import alias — workers/src/index.js:527 — for workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js's OWN handleTrustCenter, a different function from workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js's same-named handleTrustCenter despite the identical name)

frontend.evidence: CORRECTED 2026-07-11 — the prior diagnosis on this entry (2026-07-11 domain audit) was itself wrong and is superseded here, not just re-verified: it read frontend/trust-center.html's fetch('https://cyberdudebivash.in/api/trust-center') as an orphaned call to a nonexistent route, on the assumption it should have targeted workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js's /api/trust/compliance. In fact /api/trust-center is a real, live, already-wired route (workers/src/index.js:5304) resolving to workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js's OWN handleTrustCenter (a second function that happens to share its name with workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js's unrelated handleTrustCenter — confirmed by reading the actual import at workers/src/index.js:527: `handleTrustCenter as handleEnterpriseTrustCenter` is imported from workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js, not workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js). That handler's real response shape (platform_stats, compliance_status.frameworks[].{framework,status,evidence}, vulnerability_disclosure) matches frontend/trust-center.html's script field-for-field — the page was never broken. This was caught before shipping a bad 'fix' in this same session: an initial attempt to rewire the frontend to workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js's /api/trust/center + /api/trust/compliance instead (based on the flawed prior diagnosis) was reverted after directly testing the real router and finding /api/trust-center already returns real, correctly-shaped, non-empty data.

navigation.evidence: frontend/trust-center.html is linked from site navigation and genuinely renders real compliance-alignment data from workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js's handleTrustCenter — this was never actually broken (see frontend.evidence for the corrected diagnosis).

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/enterprisePortalMetricsSourcing.test.mjs (new, 2026-07-11, 3 tests) covers the real bug found and fixed this session (see notes) — not a frontend-wiring bug, a metrics-sourcing bug in the handler this page actually calls. workers/test/trustMetricsContract.test.mjs (pre-existing) covers the unrelated, separate workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js handlers this page does NOT call.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js read in full (all 6 endpoints); workers/src/index.js:527,5304-5306,7856,7865 read directly to confirm the two same-named-but-different handleTrustCenter functions and their real routes. Real bug found and fixed: getLivePlatformMetrics() computed platform_stats.security_scans (Trust Center) and platform_stats.scans_run (Enterprise Sales Kit, handleEnterpriseSalesKit — a SECOND real caller of the same buggy function, found while fixing the first) from COUNT(*) FROM service_orders — paid orders, not scans, a materially smaller number under the wrong label — and cves_in_database/cves_tracked from an always-cold COUNT(*) FROM threat_intel, instead of the canonical hydrated blend GET /api/trust/metrics already uses (handleTrustMetrics, workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js — already imported in this file for uptime only). Both real, customer/prospect-facing surfaces (public Trust Center, and the Enterprise Sales Kit sent to prospects evaluating this platform against Mandiant/CrowdStrike/etc.) could undersell real platform volume under the wrong numbers. Fixed by having getLivePlatformMetrics() call handleTrustMetrics() itself and both callers inherit the corrected values with zero call-site changes. customer_journey_complete stays false and verification.method is dynamic_api not dynamic_browser — this wave verified the real handlers directly with real stubs, not a live production browser pass; that's the natural next increment.

notes: Positive finding, not just a gap: unlike frontend/compliance-management.html (see CAP-COMP-004's notes), trustCenter.js's COMPLIANCE_FRAMEWORKS is deliberately honest — e.g. 'Not yet formally assessed against SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria' and 'no formal certification held' for ISO 27001. **Real finding, flagged but NOT fixed this session (needs an explicit decision, not a unilateral merge — matching this registry's established convention for duplicate-system findings, e.g. CAP-CRM-004/006, CAP-MKT-005):** enterprisePortalHandlers.js maintains its OWN, independent, hand-written compliance_status.frameworks list (7 frameworks, 3-state: Implemented/Aligned/Planning) that overlaps but does not match trustCenter.js's COMPLIANCE_FRAMEWORKS (9 frameworks, 2-state: aligned/partial) — and on GDPR specifically, the two DISAGREE: trustCenter.js says 'partial' ('Not yet formally assessed against GDPR 2016/679') while enterprisePortalHandlers.js says 'Aligned' ('Data minimization, consent, deletion rights implemented') for the exact same company, same framework. This is currently NOT a live customer-visible contradiction — the only frontend code that ever called trustCenter.js's /api/trust/compliance (frontend/assets/sentinel-apex-live-metrics.js and a byte-identical duplicate at frontend/assets/js/sentinel-apex-live-metrics.js) is not loaded by any <script> tag on any page in frontend/ (confirmed by repo-wide grep) — fully dead code today. It would become a live, enterprise-visible contradiction the moment either dead file is ever wired up without first reconciling the two lists. Recommend a follow-up: (1) decide which framework list and vocabulary is canonical (or maintain one shared source both handlers read), (2) either wire up or delete the two dead duplicate JS files, not both left as unreferenced dead code indefinitely.
